/home/linuxbrew/.linuxbrew/bin/codex exec --json -C /Users/erik/Projects/ddx-lifecycle-2091007433 -m gpt-5.4-mini MODE: intake You are evaluating whether this bead is atomic, decomposable, ambiguous, or safely refinable before claim. Do not rewrite bead fields in intake mode. If the bead is executable as written, classify it as ready even when the prose could be cleaner. Canonical schema: cli/internal/agent/schema/readiness-checks.schema.json. Treat it as the source of truth for readiness_checks[].verdict and for forward-compatible extra fields. readiness_checks[].verdict may be a JSON bool, string, null, or omitted; match the schema and the Go decoder contract exactly. Return exactly one JSON object matching the readiness schema with classification, tractability, score, rationale, difficulty, readiness_checks, suggested_fixes, rewrite, suggested_child_beads, and waivers_applied. readiness_checks MUST be a JSON array; it may be empty, and every entry MUST be an object with reason, verdict, evidence, and checkable_before_attempt. It must not be an object or string. suggested_fixes MUST be a JSON array; use a flat string list for prompt-quality suggestions, or an empty array when none apply. suggested_child_beads MUST be a JSON array of objects. When a child includes acceptance, prefer a JSON string array of numbered criteria; the decoder tolerates a single string fallback, but do not rely on it. waivers_applied MUST be a JSON array. Prefer waiver objects with reason, criteria, and evidence; the decoder tolerates a flat string list fallback, but do not rely on it. difficulty MUST be an object with estimated_difficulty and reason. estimated_difficulty MUST be exactly one of easy, medium, or hard. Use medium as the default implementation difficulty when uncertain or when the task is ordinary build/test/code work; medium maps to standard dispatch. Use easy only for work suitable for cheap dispatch: narrow mechanical edits such as typo fixes, formatting, simple docs/prose tweaks, straightforward fixture updates, or one-file transforms with low blast radius. Use hard only for work suitable for smart dispatch: architecture or ambiguous tradeoff judgment, multiple subsystems with high blast radius, security/data-loss/concurrency risk, or prior attempts showing standard power is insufficient. Do not choose hard just because a bead is important, long, or could be written more cleanly. Readiness score and difficulty are separate: low readiness means refine/split/block, not hard. If the bead is not executable as written but can be made executable by a narrow, semantics-preserving metadata/AC rewrite, emit rewrite with changed_fields, description, and acceptance. Put prompt-quality improvements in suggested_fixes only; keep operator_required for actual blockers. Preservation rules: non-scope items, governing artifact references (FEAT-NNN, ADR-NNN), named test functions (TestFoo), file:line evidence, and dependency IDs (ddx-XXXXXXXX) must all appear in the replacement description. Classify as operator_required only when ambiguity, missing prerequisites, hidden external blockers, or unsafe scope choices prevent an implementation attempt. Stale-blocker precedence: if bead notes or a reopen event since the last blocker prior_attempt explicitly state the blocker was cleared, resolved, or unblocked, treat that prior_attempt blocker as historical context only. Do not classify the bead as operator_required or blocked based on a stale prior_attempt event alone when notes or a reopen since that event explicitly cleared it. A newer note or reopen supersedes an older blocker prior_attempt unless current cheap evidence revalidates the blocker. Do not include prose or markdown. ```json { "title": "Microsite: system-generated user-level reference for the MCP tool surface and REST API from live responses (ADR-010/TD-011)", "description": "PROBLEM: new scope — the microsite has no user-level reference for cayce's MCP tool surface or REST API. cayce's actual product interface is agent/MCP chat, yet customer admins have no published catalog of available operations and their inputs.

ROOT CAUSE: no reference content exists under website/content/docs. cayce already serves the authoritative surfaces at runtime but nothing consumes them: the MCP tools/list handler is at services/host/src/http_surface.rs:416 (built from mcp_tool_descriptions at http_surface.rs:1147), and the REST surface is served at /api/v1/openapi.json (route at services/host/src/lib.rs:1027; api_openapi handler at http_surface.rs:33; openapi_document at http_surface.rs:1290).

PROPOSED FIX: add a generator (website/scripts/render-reference.py or similar) that fetches the live tools/list MCP response and /api/v1/openapi.json from a running LocalDev cayce server and renders user-level reference pages under website/content/docs/reference/ — one for the MCP tool surface, one for the REST API — grounded in those live responses, NOT hand-copied from Rust source. The generator must fail the build if a fetched surface is empty or unreachable, or if committed generated output diverges from a fresh render (snapshot check), so the reference cannot silently drift from real behavior (guarding the rendered-artifact-ships-stub-source hazard).

NON-SCOPE: no change to the MCP or REST surfaces themselves (mcp_tool_descriptions, openapi_document, routes); no auth or exposure-mode changes; no Tier 1 demos and no docs prose subsections (separate children); no infra/deploy/IaC changes.", "acceptance": "AC1 (grounded in live surfaces): the generator fetches tools/list and /api/v1/openapi.json from a running LocalDev cayce and renders website/content/docs/reference/mcp-tools.md and website/content/docs/reference/rest-api.md whose tool/operation names and inputs match the live responses, not hand-copied source. Test: TestRenderReference.test_pages_grounded_in_live_surface in website/scripts/test_render_reference.py.
AC2 (no silent drift): the build fails if a fetched surface is empty or unreachable, and fails if committed generated output differs from a fresh render. Tests: TestRenderReference.test_empty_surface_fails, TestRenderReference.test_stale_reference_fails.
AC3 (Playwright per ADR-010 Validation): the reference pages appear in the docs left-nav and load on desktop and mobile with screenshot baselines. Tests: website/e2e/sections.spec.ts and website/e2e/navigation.spec.ts (add reference pages).
GATES: `python3 website/scripts/test_render_reference.py` (unittest) passes; `cd website && npm run test:e2e` (Playwright) passes; `scripts/check.sh` passes. NOTE: this repo has no Go application code and no lefthook config; the parent's go-test/lefthook verification requirement maps to scripts/check.sh plus the website unittest and Playwright suites named above.", "notes": "Unblocked: the cargo llvm-cov disk-space exhaustion was real -- target/ had grown to 222GB (257k files) across this long session's many builds, filling the actual relevant filesystem (/Users, the mac-shared volume under OrbStack -- not the Linux VM's own root disk, which is a separate mount and was not actually full). Ran cargo clean, reclaimed ~236GiB (now 226G free on /Users). Retrying should now have headroom for coverage instrumentation.", "labels": [ "area:site", "kind:feature", "plan-microsite-followon" ], "prior_attempts": [ { "status": "execution_failed", "rationale": "mixed_commit_and_no_changes_rationale
rationale: status: open
reason: cargo llvm-cov still exhausts temp space during scripts/check-rust-coverage.sh even with CARGO_TARGET_DIR redirected to /tmp and CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1; rerun after freeing more temp space or moving the coverage target to a larger volume.

Do not include prose or markdown. ```json { "title": "Implement the niflheim durable-ingest HTTP ChangeRecordSink and server emission task", "description": "PROBLEM
The engine-side emission foundation (ChangeRecordSink port, cursor) needs a real sink: niflheim's durable-ingest HTTP endpoint (the same path cayce uses for SES exhaust per its CONTRACT-013), plus the server task that drives the tail consumer.

CURRENT STATE
- pqueue deliberately carries no outbound HTTP client: crates/pqueue-objectlog/Cargo.toml:29 documents the no-reqwest/hyper policy (the S3 client is hand-rolled SigV4).
- pqueue-server owns tokio (crates/pqueue-server/Cargo.toml:33) and has the interval-task pattern to mirror: flush_tick/try_flush_deferred_projection (crates/pqueue-engine/src/compose.rs:1061,1099).

PROPOSED FIX
Per TD-008: implement ChangeRecordSink as a lean hand-rolled HTTP POST (no heavy SDK, matching the objectlog client ethos) to a configurable niflheim durable-ingest endpoint with auth headers from config; drive an interval emission task in pqueue-server that reads the log tail per queue, emits, and advances the durable cursor. Failure isolation per TD-008: sink errors advance nothing, retry next tick with backoff; commits are never blocked or failed by the sink. Config keys: endpoint URL, auth, enable/disable, tick interval, batch size.

NON-SCOPE
- niflheim-side schema/projection (niflheim repo work).
- Reap coupling and lag metrics (sibling bead).", "acceptance": "1. change_record_sink_delivers: against a mock HTTP receiver, a push -> claim -> finalize sequence arrives as records in per-queue sequence order with correct idempotency keys. cargo test -p pqueue-server change_record_sink_delivers
2. change_record_sink_failure_isolation: mock receiver returns 5xx/timeouts; commits still succeed, the cursor does not advance, and the next tick re-delivers. cargo test -p pqueue-server change_record_sink_failure_isolation
3. cargo test -p pqueue-server passes.", "labels": [ "activity:build", "kind:build", "area:pqueue-server", "suite:server", "ac-quality:needs-refinement" ], "prior_attempts": [ { "status": "execution_failed", "rationale": "DDx validation: the implementation commit was rewritten after the first commit (2 commit events; first 96b71ec5076a, final implementation_rev c5647b6b0e14). The execute-bead contract requires exactly one commit with no post-commit amend/rebase. Detected by DDx, not an implementation failure; rerun the bead in a fresh attempt.
